title: Rose and geranium soy candle
subtitle: A floral soy container candle scented with rose absolute and rose geranium
excerpt: Rose absolute and rose geranium essential oil combine to produce a full, rounded floral fragrance without the single-note sweetness of rose alone. Rose geranium extends the blend and adds a green, herbal edge. The candle works in a bedroom or bathroom.
sourceNotes: Rose and geranium fragrance blending from Gracefruit fragrance-making guide (gracefruit.com, May 2026). Rose EO properties from Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014). Soy wax fragrance load rates from soy wax manufacturer datasheet.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 container candle (~180 g wax in a 200 ml glass jar)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale wax and fragrance proportionally. Keep the total fragrance at 8% of wax weight.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cure for at least 3 days before the first burn. The floral note develops and softens during the cure period.
recipe.temperatureNote: Add the fragrance at 60 to 65 degrees Celsius to preserve the lighter floral molecules.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 2 years in a cool, dark place with the lid on. Floral essential oils are generally stable in wax; keep away from direct sunlight to slow fading.

glossaryTerms.definition: The percentage of fragrance or essential oil by weight relative to the wax. This formula uses 8% fragrance load, the standard for soy container wax.
The strength and reach of fragrance from a candle. Rose essential oils have strong cold throw; the hot throw in a soy candle is softer and more diffuse.

content.text: Pure rose absolute is one of the most expensive essential oils. At full concentration, it can tip into an overpowering sweetness. Rose geranium (Pelargonium roseum) is a more affordable alternative with a similar character and a slightly greener, herbaceous edge. Blending the two stops the scent from becoming one-dimensional. This formula uses a 1:2 ratio of rose absolute to rose geranium: the combined
sits at 8% of wax weight. The
from soy wax is softer than paraffin and suits a bedroom or bathroom rather than a large open-plan room.

items.prepNote: Rose absolute; 2.8% of wax weight; the depth note
5.5% of wax weight; extends the floral note and adds a green edge
CD-12 for a 6 to 7 cm jar

content.text: Fix the wick to the base of the jar and centre using a centring bar or two chopsticks. Melt the soy wax in the pouring pitcher over a simmering-water bath. Remove from heat when the wax is fully liquid. Allow to cool to 60 to 65 degrees Celsius. Stir in both essential oils. Pour slowly into the jar. Leave undisturbed at room temperature for at least 8 hours before handling. Trim the wick to 5 mm before the first burn.
Allow the melt pool to reach the jar edge on the first burn, which takes around 3 hours. This prevents wax memory tunnelling. Never burn for more than 4 hours at a time.
2 years in a cool, dark place with the lid on. Keep away from direct sunlight, which bleaches the colour and fades the fragrance faster.
